steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: "contains three signature elements of Gwendolyn Brooks's poetic ___" — a complete independent clause that announces a list.

After the blank: a list of three elements introduced by "terse,".

This is the classic setup for a colon, which introduces a list/elaboration after an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
style: terse, uses a colon to introduce the list — correct.

A (period) breaks the sentence with a fragment. B (comma) is a comma splice that fails to signal a list. D (semicolon) is misused; semicolons separate two independent clauses or list items containing internal commas, but the part after "style;" isn't an independent clause.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
